2. Historic Townhouse – Priced at CAD $750K  
   • Style & Description: This beautiful 180m² period property retains charming Victorian features, including original woodwork and period-specific details that have been sensitively restored.  
   • Key Accessibility Features: It is situated in a cultural district with gentle access ramps and adapted entryways supporting ease of mobility.  
   • Maintenance & Notes: While the property has been well maintained, its heritage status means that some aspects require periodic specialist maintenance. Like the Family House, the quoted price is exclusively for the property purchase, with additional ancillary costs to be applied separately.

Additional Cost Clarifications:  
• Legal Services: You can expect legal fees in the range of CAD $800 (Basic Legal Package) to CAD $1200 (Premium Legal Package) based on the level of service required.  
• Property Survey Fees: These typically range between CAD $300 (Basic Survey) and CAD $650 (Full Structural Survey) depending on the depth of inspection needed.  
• Moving Services: Standard Moving services are approximately CAD $650, though this may vary with specific requirements or additional services.

External Agent: 
Below is a comprehensive overview addressing your queries:

1. Financing Solutions for These Properties:
   • Home Equity vs. Cash Purchase:
     – When it comes to properties like the Family House or Historic Townhouse, many buyers often explore home equity options if they already own a property. This can allow leveraging existing assets to secure a competitive mortgage. Terms typically involve a traditional mortgage (about 80% LTV) or specialized schemes tailored for downsizers.
     – A cash purchase remains an appealing option, especially in competitive markets, eliminating ongoing financing costs. In our case, if you opt for a cash purchase, you may expect a legal processing fee of around CAD $500 rather than arranging a mortgage.
     
   • Common Terms and Conditions:
     – For home equity based financing, banks usually require a credit check, proof of income, and an appraisal of the current and new property. 
     – Down payment requirements typically range from 10% (for cash-in-hand buyers) to 20% for financed deals.
     
   • Recommended Financing Partners:
     – We regularly work with local mortgage specialists and national banks that have a strong track record with balanced portfolios in this price range. While I can provide specific contacts upon request, these institutions have an established history of working seamlessly with our clients.
     – Additionally, some credit unions offer attractive terms for home equity loans that might support a downsizing strategy, so it may be worthwhile to compare their offers.

2. Recommended Service Providers:
   • Legal Services:
     – For legal matters, many of our clients opt for either the Basic Legal Package (CAD $800) or the Premium Legal Package (CAD $1200) depending on their need for due diligence and swift processing. Our agency has established relationships with reputable local law firms that are well-versed in property conveyancing.
     
   • Property Surveys:
     – Depending on the property's age and condition, a Basic Survey (approx. CAD $300) or a Full Structural Survey (around CAD $650) is recommended. We also have contacts with surveyors who have experience with both modern and historic properties.
     
   • Moving Services:
     – For moving, Standard Moving services (typically CAD $650) are a common choice. If your timeline is tight or if there’s a need for extra care (especially with historic properties), a Premium Moving service (about CAD $1200) might be beneficial.
     – We can also recommend specific providers who have delivered excellent service to our other clients in similar transitions.

To further streamline the process and ensure that all details—from
